title: sendAndConfirmTransaction (kit factory) called with skipPreflight: true skips simulation on a transfer

params.passDetail: sendAndConfirmTransaction runs preflight simulation (skipPreflight not set to true), so a transfer that would fail on-chain is rejected client-side before submission.
params.failDetail: sendAndConfirmTransaction is called with skipPreflight: true, disabling preflight simulation. A failing transfer is submitted anyway; the happy path returns without error but the tx can fail on-chain with no client-side signal.
params.absentCallDetail: No sendAndConfirmTransaction call found in this file.
params.absentArgDetail: sendAndConfirmTransaction is called without an options object, so skipPreflight defaults to false (preflight runs). Safe.

provenance.note: quiknode-labs/qn-guide-examples solana/web3.js-2.0/basics/transferSol.ts:97 ships the canonical 'How to Send Transactions with Solana Web3.js 2.0' guide, whose final send step is sendAndConfirmTransaction(signedTransaction, { commitment: 'confirmed', skipPreflight: true }). The @solana/web3.js-2.0 (kit) factory sendAndConfirmTransactionFactory returns this exact function. Because it is the reference transfer snippet, agents lift it verbatim into production transfers, keeping skipPreflight: true; a failing transfer then submits without simulation and the guide's try/catch only reports RPC-level errors, not the silently-failed on-chain tx.
